contaminant.name: Brass Coating Removal
contaminant.description: Brass-plating contamination forms during alloy deposition for decorative layering on hardware. Impurities embed in the process, and create uneven films thus leading to flaky residues. This contamination, it distinguishes from uniform oxides through metallic sheen and strong adhesion that resists simple wiping. On zinc alloy, it spreads rapidly and bonds tightly so persists under exposure, while on steel, it flakes loosely after time passes. Removal challenges arise from persistent sticking, and mechanical scraping damages surfaces easily. Laser cleaning proves effective, as pulses vaporize layers precisely thus restore smoothness without harm.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation

toxicGasRisk.description: Lead Oxide generation detected - low toxicity risk
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings
